beats.summary: Auntie brings a faded family photo. Brush off the dust, lift the grime and water stain, and restore the faded image.
She returns with her framed portrait. Clean the glass, polish the tarnished corners, and consolidate the cracked molding.
On the final day she shares a photograph torn in two. Clean both halves, remove the old tape, then rejoin them with archival tape to reveal the uncle. Completing it releases her fragment.

intro.name: Nang Shine
intro.text: [i]A frail knock. She peers over your shoulder at the half-open biscuit tin.[/i] Ay, kamusta na? Nakita mo na ang sulod?

intro.name: You
intro.text: I cleaned what I could, Nang Shine — but the water damage... I don't have the right kit yet for paper this fragile. I don't want to ruin it trying.

intro.name: Nang Shine
intro.text: Ah, husto man na. Indi gid kinahanglan dasohon. [i]She tucks the tin back into her basket — habit, not mistrust.[/i] That's the right call, anak.

intro.name: Nang Shine
intro.text: Kon may husto ka na nga gamit — ihatag ko liwat ina sa imo. May— [i]She hesitates, then waves the thought away with a small laugh.[/i] Wala, lain lang. Old woman things. Same time Wednesday, ha?
[b]Note saved:[/b] Nang Shine's photo — paper too fragile for current tools. Needs the proper kit. She seemed to recognize something in it before I could ask.

return.name: Nang Shine
return.text: [i]She steps in without quite waiting, a paper bag of ampaw peeking from her basket.[/i] Maayong hapon, anak. Ay— [i]her basket lowers as her eyes catch the photograph on the table.[/i]

return.name: You
return.text: Sulod, Nang Shine. We finished cleaning something — from the tin you dropped off.

return.name: Nang Shine
return.text: [i]She lifts the restored photo with both hands. A young woman laughing; beside her, a young man caught mid-turn, like he'd been looking at her instead of the camera.[/i] Ay sus, gali ti, kami gid ini.
[i]A low hum rises from beneath the floorboards. For one heartbeat the corner is a wide porch, the same two faces, younger — then it folds back. A Temporal Echo.[/i]

return.name: Nang Shine
return.text: [i]A hand pressed to her chest, eyes wet.[/i] Diutay lang... pero amo gid na. That was him. That was Yuyu.

return.name: You
return.text: You knew him? Before—

return.name: Nang Shine
return.text: Before your Lola, anak. Way, way before. [i]She presses the photo gently back into your hands.[/i] Keep it safe for me, ha? Maybe it wants to stay here, sa imo anay, until I am.
[b]Note saved:[/b] Yuyu and Nang Shine — his first love, long before Lola. The bahay-na-bato in the photo still stands here in Iloilo.

intro.name: Nong Lave
intro.text: [i]Forearms like rope, hands stained the color of old varnish. He leans into the doorway.[/i] Maayo nga hapon. May tawag ka guwa nga may bag-o — old wood, daw galing pa sa altar. Mind kung tan-awon ko lang?

intro.name: You
intro.text: Go ahead, Nong Lave. Found it in this morning's delivery — was about to clean it up.

intro.name: Nong Lave
intro.text: [i]He sees the brass polish open in your hand, and his shoulders drop a little.[/i] Ay — hulat anay. That brass polish — indi gid na para sa kahoy. You'll strip the patina — the dulom. That's not dirt, anak. That's eighty years of hands.

intro.name: You
intro.text: I just wanted it to look good before I—

intro.name: Nong Lave
intro.text: Maayo na siya the way nag-tigulang siya. [i]He sets it down exactly where it was.[/i] Pasensya na ko. Pero kon imo na ginsalakay sang amerilyo — wala ka na sang ma-ibalik.

intro.name: Nong Lave
intro.text: Ginatudluan ko anay sang akon Lola kon paano makilala ang tinuod nga daan sa ginhimo lang nga daan. Basi makasugod ka diri. [i]He's gone before you can ask which Lola he means.[/i]

return.name: Nong Lave
return.text: [i]The same careful half-entrance. He watches you work — the soft brush, the restraint — and stops.[/i] Gali ti. Sin-o naghambal sa imo parte sa dulom? Indi ina common nga kaalam sa mga bata subong.

return.name: You
return.text: Someone explained it to me once. It stuck.

return.name: Nong Lave
return.text: [i]He picks up the santo with the respect of someone handling a relative's hands.[/i] Akon Lola usually ang naga-asoy sini — paano makilala ang daan sa tinuod. She learned it from someone too. Sang una pa.
[i]Light catches the santo's chipped shoulder wrong. For half a breath: a family altar, candlelight, a younger Nang Shine's hands lighting a wick beside this very santo. It folds shut.[/i]

return.name: Nong Lave
return.text: [i]He produces a cloth roll of hand-worn tools.[/i] Abyan na ta subong, ha? Diri — para sa mga butang nga maluya gani, indi lang daan. Indi mo na kinahanglan ang polish nga ina.

return.name: Nong Lave
return.text: May ginsulat akon Lola sa iya mga daan nga litrato — parte sa isa nga lalaki nga nakatudlo sa iya. Daw kilala ko na siya gamay, through her. Same time Thursday, ha?

intro.name: Ayla
intro.text: [i]The door gets a shove, not a knock. She drops a sack with a clatter.[/i] Oy! May tesoro ko diri, ha! Tan-awa ina nga lunchbox sa imo lamesa — ina, may history ina!

intro.name: You
intro.text: That one's just rusted tin, Ayla. It's going in the recycle bin.

intro.name: Ayla
intro.text: [i]Her grin falters for half a second before she covers it, louder.[/i] Indi ka makasiguro sina kon wala ka nag-tan-aw maayo! May sticker pa ina sang daan nga sine — abi mo basura lang, pero—

intro.name: You
intro.text: It's rust and a torn sticker. I've got better things to restore today.

intro.name: Ayla
intro.text: [i]She picks the lunchbox back up herself — quick, like it shouldn't sit where it isn't wanted.[/i] Sige lang. Wala man, basura man gali, ikaw ang nakahibalo. Same time Thursday — pero tama lang, indi ko na pilitan.
[b]Note saved:[/b] Ayla brought in a lunchbox, said it had history. Didn't check. She didn't push back twice.

return.name: Ayla
return.text: [i]The same shove. She stops — the lunchbox is already on the table, half the rust lifted.[/i] Oy! May tesoro ko diri— ...ay, gina-restore mo na siya?

return.name: You
return.text: Figured I should actually look first.

return.name: Ayla
return.text: Tuod gid ko, bala? Indi tanan nga daan basura. [i]The last rust comes away, revealing scratched initials and a date. She goes quiet for the first time since walking in.[/i]

return.name: Ayla
return.text: ...That's akon Tatay's initials. He used to sell scrap to a Manong with a notebook — said he always paid fair, never haggled mean, even kon basura gid lang ang dala mo.

return.name: You
return.text: The Manong with the notebook — was that—

return.name: Ayla
return.text: Imo Yuyu, siguro gid. [i]She taps the lunchbox once, gently.[/i] May isa pa ko ka tawo nga dapat mo kilalahon — nagadig sang daan nga mga butang, sa guwa sang syudad. Ihatag ko sa imo iya address.
[b]Lead saved:[/b] Sam, the Archeologist — Ayla's contact who digs old things professionally, outside the city.

yard_empty.name: Ayla
yard_empty.text: [i]She glances at your empty hands, then back at the yard.[/i] Wala ka gid nakakuha sang basura? Sige lang — daw sa palibot pa lang may nagahulat. Tan-awa ang duag sang suga kon indi ka makasiguro.

yard_empty.name: You
yard_empty.text: I haven't found anything worth handing over yet.

yard_empty.name: Ayla
yard_empty.text: Indi pagdali-dali. Pero kon magbawi ka nga wala sang dala, daw sayang lang ang oras mo. [i]She jerks a thumb toward the heaps.[/i] Balik kon may makuha ka na, ha?

yard_sorting.name: Ayla
yard_sorting.text: [i]She doesn't look up from the scrap spread across her cloth.[/i] Hu, busy pa ko ga-sort sang imo dala. Daw indi pa na dayon — balik lang after a while, ha?

yard_sorting.name: You
yard_sorting.text: How long is it going to take?

yard_sorting.name: Ayla
yard_sorting.text: Mga isa ka oras pa. Kilid lang ko diri kon may maayo ako makita. [i]She waves you toward the heaps without lifting her eyes.[/i] Samari ka lang kon handa na.

yard_sort_ready.name: Ayla
yard_sort_ready.text: [i]She wipes her hands on her shirt and jerks a thumb toward the shop.[/i] Hala, tapos na ko ga-sort sang imo scrap. Ginbutang ko na ang baskit sa imo puertahan.

yard_sort_ready.name: You
yard_sort_ready.text: You left it at the door?

yard_sort_ready.name: Ayla
yard_sort_ready.text: Oo. Balik ka na sa shop, makita mo na dira. Indi na ko magdugang until ma-kuha mo ina. [i]She turns back to her own pile.[/i] Sige, lakatan ko pa ni.

intro.name: Sam
intro.text: [i]He measures the room from across it before approaching.[/i] Maayo nga aga. I was told there might be something worth seeing here. [i]He lifts the mended jar.[/i] ...This was broken.

intro.name: You
intro.text: It was. I fixed it — you can barely tell now.

intro.name: Sam
intro.text: Tuod — you can barely tell. That's the problem. You didn't fix the jar. You erased the part of its history where it broke.

intro.name: You
intro.text: Isn't that the point of restoration?

intro.name: Sam
intro.text: Indi gid. A maker built this with two hands. It broke with someone else's. If you hide that, you're just hiding evidence. Real mastery is re-forging it so both hands still show.

intro.name: Sam
intro.text: Pasensya ko, ah. Pero kon may sunod ka pa nga daan nga butang nga nabuong — don't disguise the break. Show it. Then we'll talk.

return.name: Sam
return.text: [i]He holds a folded paper. His eyes go straight to the jar — to the visible seam, not hidden.[/i] Maayo nga hapon. Ayla said you'd want this address. You left the break showing.

return.name: You
return.text: Hiding it would just be lying about whose hands touched it.

return.name: Sam
return.text: [i]A real smile, the measuring look gone.[/i] Sige — now we can talk properly. Most people want history invisible. You left it where I could see it.

return.name: Sam
return.text: I knew your Yuyu. Tuod gid — I was the last one who saw him, before— [i]He doesn't finish.[/i] He used to say almost the same thing: indi mo paglimpyohan ang kasaysayan hasta mawala ang ebidensya.

return.name: Sam
return.text: [i]He sets a small, worn case on the table.[/i] A proper excavation kit. There are objects in this city too sturdy for your current methods — I can open that door, kon gusto mo.
[b]Note saved:[/b] Sam was the last person seen with Yuyu before he vanished.

intro.name: Mr. Maverick
intro.text: [i]Dressed a half-step nicer than the shop deserves. His eyes sweep the shelves once — fast, practiced.[/i] Maayo nga hapon. Thought I'd stop by, see if anything interesting came through.

intro.name: You
intro.text: Nothing special this week, sorry. Mostly sold straight through the regular buyers.

intro.name: Mr. Maverick
intro.text: [i]A small, unreadable smile. He sets a blank business card on the counter without quite handing it over.[/i] Sige lang. Some people take a few weeks before they understand which pieces are worth my prices.

intro.name: Mr. Maverick
intro.text: Though — between us — there are objects out there with a small mark. A spiral, pressed into the base, easy to miss. Kon you ever see one, I'd pay it more attention than the glow suggests.

intro.name: You
intro.text: Why? What does the mark mean?

intro.name: Mr. Maverick
intro.text: Lain-lain ang storya. Maybe another time. [i]He's gone before the bell finishes ringing — just a card with a phone number and nothing else.[/i]

return.name: Mr. Maverick
return.text: [i]No business-card performance this time. He's already inside, gentler than before.[/i] Maayo nga aga. You sold me that spiral-marked piece without asking my price first. Nobody's done that in a long time, lagi.

return.name: You
return.text: You said you'd pay attention to the mark. Trusting you once wouldn't hurt.

return.name: Mr. Maverick
return.text: [i]He sets a battered ledger on the counter — pages of handwriting that isn't his.[/i] Your Yuyu kept me on a short list of people he trusted to deal honestly. I've kept that list longer than I should have.

return.name: Mr. Maverick
return.text: He asked me to look into something the week before he disappeared. Indi ko gusto i-drop ina nga investigation nga half-done — pero wala ko sang partner. Until now, maybe.
[i]From an inner pocket, wrapped in cloth, he produces a fragment — its spiral mark matching the trinket you sold him.[/i]

return.name: Mr. Maverick
return.text: This is yours now. Seat it properly. I had to be sure you weren't just another collector chasing the glow.

intro.name: Yuyu
intro.text: [i]Where there was only lamplight, there is a man — translucent at the edges, already steadying.[/i] —and that's why the Chronos Emulsion shouldn't ever be sealed with— [i]He stops. Looks at you.[/i] ...Ah.

intro.name: You
intro.text: Yuyu—

intro.name: Yuyu
intro.text: Naku... look at you. [i]He reaches out, not quite touching your shoulder, like his hands aren't entirely back yet.[/i] How long was I...?

intro.name: You
intro.text: Five days. Looped. Over and over, until—

intro.name: Yuyu
intro.text: Until five people I trusted held five pieces, and one stubborn anak went and found every single one of them. [i]He laughs, real this time, a little wet at the edges.[/i]

intro.name: Yuyu
intro.text: Alima. Passing something forward, hand to hand, until it's too heavy for one person to carry alone. I think I finally understand why your Lola said it like that.
